arc: p10-centerline-gate: octoloop v1 was REJECTED (vacuous+non-idempotent on real data). v2 (codex xhigh) rewrote it; this artifact is the orchestrator adversarial verification that cleared v2 for commit, plus the orchestrator-added name_map plumbing (my packet signature omitted it; block_topology.py establishes the convention).

vacuity.note: v1 found ZERO centerlines (scanned only top-level entities); v2 scans block_definitions[].def_entities.

four_v1_defects_fixed.vertical_bucket: FIXED (undirected angle atan2(dy,dx) mod pi; unit test test_vertical_wall_pair_is_covered_and_translation_fails)
four_v1_defects_fixed.phantom_pairs: FIXED (adjacent-offset pairing only; unit tests three_collinear + room_width_gap assert 2 not C(n,2))

rebuild_fidelity_with_name_map.interpretation: WALL (평면도) blocks are PERFECT (0 mismatch, 1536 non-vacuous centerlines). Residual FAIL is isolated to *D associative-dimension blocks, where the gate extracts spurious 'centerlines' from parallel dimension/extension lines in the 10-400 band. This is a CALLER-SCOPING concern (aim the gate at wall-bearing block definitions), not a gate-logic bug -- the gate correctly reports that dimension geometry differs. Harness integration should scope to wall blocks or accept dimension-block deltas.
